heading: Little Scholars Child Development Center; employee compensation and personnel matters
label: 162b Little Scholars Child Development Center; employee compensation and personnel matters

L. 116–159“(1)the authority of the Library of Congress to reimburse the Little Scholars Child Development Center at the Library of Congress under section 19004 of the CARES Act ( note; ) shall remain in effect with respect to salaries incurred until the termination of the public health emergency declared pursuant to section 319 of the Public Health Service Act () resulting from the COVID–19 pandemic; and2 U.S.C. 162b134 Stat. 57842 U.S.C. 247d“(2)the authority of the Government Accountability Office to reimburse the Tiny Findings Child Development Center under section 19009 of the CARES Act () shall remain in effect with respect to salaries incurred until the termination of the public health emergency declared pursuant to section 319 of the Public Health Service Act () resulting from the COVID–19 pandemic.”134 Stat. 57942 U.S.C. 247d(3) [Amended , set out as a note under 1816b of this title.]section 19005(a) of Pub. B, title IX, § 19004134 Stat. 578Pub. L. 116–260, div. O, title XI, § 1101(b)(1)134 Stat. 2156Mar. 27, 2020Dec. 27, 2020“(a).—ReimbursementsDuring the period beginning on the date of enactment of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2021 [] and ending on the termination date of the public health emergency declared pursuant to section 319 of the Public Health Service Act () resulting from the COVID–19 pandemic, the Library of Congress shall reimburse the Little Scholars Child Development Center for expenses, due to measures taken in the Capitol complex to combat coronavirus, as calculated under subsection (b) and from amounts in the appropriations account ‘Library of Congress—Salaries and Expenses’.Dec. 27, 202042 U.S.C. 247d“(b).—AmountThe amount of the reimbursement under this section for each month of the period described in subsection (a) shall be equal to the difference between—“(1)the lesser of—“(A)the amount of the operating costs (including payroll, general, and administrative expenses) of the Center for such month; or“(B)$118,500; and“(2)the amount of tuition payments collected by the Center for such month.”[For definition of “coronavirus” as used in , set out above, see , set out below.]section 19004 of Pub. L. 116–136section 23005 of Pub. L. 116–136

Definition, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–136, div. B, title XIII, § 23005134 Stat. 614Mar. 27, 2020“In this Act [div. B of , see Tables for classification], the term ‘coronavirus’ means SARS–CoV–2 or another coronavirus with pandemic potential.”Pub. L. 116–136

children.heading: Election of coverage; creditable service; qualification for survivor annuities and disability benefits; contributions to thrift savings plan; certification concerning creditable service
children.label: a Election of coverage; creditable service; qualification for survivor annuities and disability benefits; contributions to thrift savings plan; certification concerning creditable service
A
children.text: is employed by the Library of Congress Child Development Center (known as the “Little Scholars Child Development Center”, in this section referred to as the “Center”) established under section 205(g)(1) of the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 1991; and

children.label: i
children.text: 60 days after ; orDecember 21, 2000

children.label: ii
children.text: 60 days after the date the individual begins such employment.

children.label: A
children.text: Any individual described under paragraph (1) may be credited, under for service as an employee of the Center before , if such employee makes a payment of the deposit under section 8411(f)(2) of such title without application of section 8411(b)(3) of such title.section 8411 of title 5December 21, 2000

children.label: B
children.text: An individual described under paragraph (1) shall be credited under for any service as an employee of the Center on or after , if such employee has such amounts deducted and withheld from his pay as determined by the Office of Personnel Management which would be deducted and withheld from the basic pay of an employee under .section 8411 of title 5section 8422 of title 5December 21, 2000

Notwithstanding any other provision of this subsection, any service performed by an individual described under paragraph (1) as an employee of the Center is deemed to be civilian service creditable under for purposes of qualifying for survivor annuities and disability benefits under subchapters IV and V of chapter 84 of such title, if such individual makes payment of an amount, determined by the Office of Personnel Management, which would have been deducted and withheld from the basic pay of such individual if such individual had been an employee subject to for such period so credited, together with interest thereon.section 8411 of title 5section 8422 of title 5

An individual described under paragraph (1) shall be deemed an employee for purposes of chapter 84 of title 5, including subchapter III of such title, and may make contributions under section 8432 of such title effective for the first applicable pay period beginning on or after the date such individual elects coverage under this section.1So in original. Probably should be “chapter,”.1
The Office of Personnel Management shall accept the certification of the Librarian of Congress concerning creditable service for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Health insurance coverage
children.label: b Health insurance coverage
children.text: Any individual who is employed by the Center on or after the date of enactment of this Act [], shall be deemed an employee under for purposes of health insurance coverage under chapter 89 of such title. An individual who is an employee of the Center on the date of enactment of this Act may elect coverage under this subsection before the 60th day after the date of enactment of this Act, and during such periods as determined by the Office of Personnel Management for employees of the Center employed after such date.December 21, 2000section 8901(1) of title 5

children.heading: Life insurance coverage
children.label: c Life insurance coverage
children.text: An individual who is employed by the Center shall be deemed an employee under for purposes of life insurance coverage under chapter 87 of such title.section 8701(a) of title 5

children.heading: Government contributions by Librarian from available appropriations
children.label: d Government contributions by Librarian from available appropriations
children.text: Government contributions for individuals receiving benefits under this section, as computed under sections 8423, 8432, 8708, and 8906 shall be made by the Librarian of Congress from any appropriations available to the Library of Congress.2So in original. Probably should be followed by “of title 5”.2

children.heading: Payroll and personnel functions of Library of Congress
children.label: e Payroll and personnel functions of Library of Congress

children.text: process payroll for Center employees, including making deductions and withholdings from the pay of employees in the amounts determined under sections 8422, 8432, 8707, and 8905 of title 5;
maintain appropriate personnel and payroll records for Center employees, and transmit appropriate information and records to the Office of Personnel Management; and
transmit funds for Government and employee contributions under this section to the Office of Personnel Management.

except as provided in subsection (g), pay to the Library of Congress funds sufficient to cover the gross salary and the employer’s share of taxes under for Center employees, in amounts computed by the Library of Congress;section 3111 of title 26
as required by the Library of Congress, reimburse the Library of Congress for reasonable administrative costs incurred under subsection (e)(1);
comply with regulations and procedures prescribed by the Librarian of Congress for administration of this section;
maintain appropriate records on all Center employees, as required by the Librarian of Congress; and
consult with the Librarian of Congress on the administration and implementation of this section.

children.heading: Reimbursement for certain compensation
children.label: g Reimbursement for certain compensation
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1) of subsection (f), in the case of expenses described in such paragraph which are attributable to the compensation of the Executive Director and Deputy Executive Director of the Center, the Librarian of Congress may reimburse the Center for such expenses from amounts appropriated or otherwise made available for salaries and expenses of the Library of Congress.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: h Regulations
children.text: The Librarian of Congress may prescribe regulations to carry out this section.
